data.title: Fiera Capital sees lower AUM for quarter ended December 31
data.description:
Fiera Capital (FRRPF) (FSZ:CA) on Thursday announced preliminary assets under management of ~C$164.1B as of December 31, 2025, down from C$166.9B at September 30, 2025.

By distribution channel, Institutional logged an AUM of C$93.6B, Financial Intermediaries of C$56.7B, and Private Wealth of C$13.7B.

The company is set to issue its fourth-quarter financial results on Thursday, February 26, 2026, before markets open.

data.title: Comcast in charts: Domestic wireless lines grow steadily, up ~19% Y/Y in Q4
PublicSquare names Dusty Wunderlich as CEO
data.description:
• PSQ Holdings (PSQH) has appointed Dusty Wunderlich as CEO, effective immediately, as the company continues its transition to core fintech businesses, including credit and payments.
  • Wunderlich was recently appointed chairman of the board of PublicSquare and will remain in that role. As part of this transition, Michael Seifert has stepped down as CEO and resigned from the board, effective January 27, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Manpower beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• Manpower press release (MAN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.92 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $4.7B (+6.8% Y/Y) beats by $70M.
  • On a constant currency basis, revenues increased 1% compared to the prior year period and, on an organic constant currency basis, revenues increased 2% compared to the prior year period.
  • "We anticipate diluted earnings per share in the first quarter will be between $0.45 and $0.55 (vs. $0.48 consensus), which includes an estimated favorable currency impact of 6 cents and a 43.0% effective tax rate."

data.title: Unicycive granted FDA review for resubmitted marketing application for kidney disease drug
data.description:
Unicycive Therapeutics (UNCY) announced on Thursday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) has accepted its resubmitted New Drug Application ((NDA)) for its kidney disease therapy oxylanthanum carbonate ((OLC)).

In late June, the FDA declined to approve the oral phosphate binder designed to target hyperphosphatemia in patients with chronic kidney disease on dialysis, citing issues at a third-party manufacturer.

The company resubmitted the NDA in December after its third-party manufacturer took steps to regain FDA compliance.

The Los Altos, California-based biotech said that the regulator has set a target action date of June 27, 2026, after determining that the OLC resubmission is a Class II complete response, which requires a six-month review period.

Additionally, citing unaudited financials, the company reported $41.3M in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments as of the end of 2025. Unicycive (UNCY) added that the liquidity position could support potential launch activities for OLC and cash runway into 2027.

data.title: Tractor Supply falls after comparable sales miss expectations
data.description:
Tractor Supply (TSCO) fell in early trading on Thursday after falling short of estimates with its fourth-quarter earnings report.

Sales rose 3.3% year-over-year to $3.9 billion. The increase in net sales was driven by new store openings, the growth in comparable store sales, and the contribution from Allivet. 

Comparable store sales increased 0.3%, reflecting comparable average ticket growth of 0.3%. The retailer noted that continued strength in consumable, usable, and edible products was partially offset by the lack of emergency-response-related demand and ongoing pressure in discretionary categories, including big-ticket products. The comparable sales tally was well below the consensus expectation of analysts of +3.0%.

Tractor Supply's (TSCO) gross margin rate dipped 10 basis points from a year ago to 35.1% of sales. Gross margin was impacted by ongoing cost management initiatives that were mostly offset by higher tariffs, incremental promotional activity, and increased delivery-related transportation costs.

Operating income decreased 6.5% to $297.7 million during the quarter. EPS was reported at $0.43 vs. $0.46 consensus and $0.44 a year ago.

The company opened 31 new Tractor Supply stores and one Petsense by Tractor Supply store in the fourth quarter.

Looking ahead, Tractor Supply (TSCO) sees full-year sales growth of +4% to +6%. EPS of $2.13 to $2.23 (midpoint $2.18) is anticipated vs. the consensus estimate of $2.30. "As we move into 2026, we are prepared to operate with discipline across a range of economic conditions. With much of our strategic foundation now in place, we remain focused on executing our Life Out Here 2030 strategic initiatives and delivering a more consistent performance profile over time," updated CEO Hal Lawton. 

Shares of Tractor Supply (TSCO) were down 4.8% in premarket trading to $52.51. The 52-week high for the stock is $63.99.

data.title: Altria aims for 'progressive' dividend rate growth
data.description:
Altria Group (MO) dipped in early trading on Thursday after falling slightly short of profit estimates with its fourth-quarter earnings report. 

Net revenue decreased 2.1% during the quarter to $5.8 billion, primarily driven by lower net revenues in the smokeable products segment. 

In the smokeable products segment, revenue decreased 2.7%, primarily driven by lower shipment volume and higher promotional investments, partially offset by higher pricing. Revenues net of excise taxes decreased 1.1%. The smokeable products segment reported domestic cigarette shipment volume decreased 7.9%, primarily driven by the industry’s decline rate (impacted by the continued growth of illicit e-vapor products and discretionary income pressures on ANCs) and trade inventory movements.

Oral tobacco product revenue increased 2.0%, primarily driven by higher pricing, partially offset by lower shipment volume and a higher percentage of on! Shipment volume relative to MST versus the prior year (mix change). Revenues net of excise taxes increased 2.9%.

Adjusted diluted EPS was level with last year's mark at $1.30, driven by a lower adjusted tax rate and fewer shares outstanding, offset by lower adjusted OCI. The EPS tally missed the consensus estimate of $1.32

"2025 was a year of continued momentum for Altria, marked by strong financial performance, strategic progress across our smoke-free portfolio, new relationships supporting our long-term growth goals, and significant cash returns to shareholders," stated CEO Billy Gifford. 

Looking ahead, Altria Group (MO) sees 2026 adjusted EPS of $5.56 to $5.72 (midpoint $5.64) vs. the consensus estimate of $5.59. The EPS forecast represents a growth rate of 2.5% to 5.5% from a base of $5.42 in 2025.

Altira (MO) also issued its 2028 enterprise goals. The company aims to deliver a mid-single-digit adjusted diluted EPS compounded annual growth rate in 2028 from a $4.871 base in 2022. Through 2025, Altria (MO) delivered a reported diluted EPS CAGR of 8.9% and an adjusted diluted EPS CAGR of 3.6% from the 2022 base. In addition, Altria  (MO) aims for a progressive dividend goal targeting mid-single-digit dividend per share growth annually through 2028. "In 2025, we increased our dividend by 3.9%, marking the 60th increase in the past 56 years. Future dividend payments remain subject to the discretion of our board," highlighted the company.

Shares of Altria (MO) edged 0.3% lower in premarket trading to $63.13 vs. the 52-week range of $50.08 to $68.60. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 6.7%.
